 {"id":"br-aazao.9.2","title":"E2E: comprehensive HTTP transport test covering all 36 tools and 33 resources","description":"The current test_http.sh has 47 assertions. Expand to cover:
- All 36 tools via HTTP POST /mcp/
- All 33 resources via resources/read
- Bearer auth: with token → 200, without → 401, wrong token → 403
- Content-Type validation: non-JSON → 415
- Large payload handling: body > 1MB → appropriate response
- Concurrent requests: 20 parallel curl calls → all succeed
Real HTTP server, real curl requests.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T14:08:56.069942309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:13:41.949783773Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:13:41.949783773Z","source_repo":".","deleted_at":"2026-04-03T01:13:41.949732427Z","deleted_by":"br-rebuild","delete_reason":"rebuild: not in JSONL","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","http","real-path","transport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.9.2","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:31:08.107524888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-aazao.9.2","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:08:56.069942309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-aazao.9.3","title":"E2E: auth matrix (bearer, JWT/JWKS, no-auth, localhost bypass)","description":"Dedicated auth E2E matrix:
- Bearer token: correct → 200, wrong → 401/403, missing → 401
- JWT: valid kid → 200, expired JWT → 401, wrong kid → 401
- No-auth mode (--no-auth flag): all requests succeed without token
- Localhost bypass (HTTP_ALLOW_LOCALHOST_UNAUTHENTICATED=true): localhost → ok, non-localhost → denied
- Auth on resources vs tools: same enforcement
Real server with different auth configs per test case.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-03-18T14:09:07.398077005Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:13:41.106070043Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:13:41.106070043Z","source_repo":".","deleted_at":"2026-04-03T01:13:41.106001084Z","deleted_by":"br-rebuild","delete_reason":"rebuild: not in JSONL","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auth","e2e","real-path","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.9.3","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:09:07.398077005Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-ah24m","title":"Config should bound AM_ARCHIVE_MAINTENANCE_INTERVAL_SECS","description":"Review of ea425d56 found archive maintenance interval parsing accepts arbitrary u64 values in Config::from_env. The worker applies a 60s lower bound later, but the parsed Config surface can still expose 0 or extremely large intervals to other callers/diagnostics, making the effective maintenance cadence inconsistent and allowing accidental practical disablement without AM_ARCHIVE_MAINTENANCE_DISABLED.\n\nRepro:\n1. Set AM_ARCHIVE_MAINTENANCE_INTERVAL_SECS=0 or 999999999999.\n2. Call mcp_agent_mail_core::Config::from_env().archive_maintenance_interval_secs.\n3. Observe the raw unbounded value instead of a documented bounded cadence.\n\nAcceptance criteria:\n- Config::from_env clamps AM_ARCHIVE_MAINTENANCE_INTERVAL_SECS to a documented sane range, at minimum preserving the maintenance worker's 60s lower bound.\n- Add a focused config unit test for low and oversized values.\n- Keep AM_ARCHIVE_MAINTENANCE_DISABLED as the explicit disable switch.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:49:55.437498751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T13:53:55.022023750Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T13:53:55.021443643Z","close_reason":"Clamping already applied by another agent; added 4 focused unit tests verifying low/high clamp, valid passthrough, and disabled-via-env. Commit ebe56338.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","maintenance","review"]}
-{"id":"br-atbuq","title":"concurrency: dispatch timeout leaves blocking tool work running","description":"Review finding in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs:9983-10055. HttpState::dispatch wraps asupersync::runtime::spawn_blocking in a hard timeout. asupersync spawn_blocking documents soft cancellation: dropping/timeout of the returned future discards the result but the blocking closure continues. Repro: configure a short HTTP request timeout, force a tools/call to block in SQLite/git work past hard_timeout_secs, observe the HTTP caller receives a timeout while dispatch_inner continues running and holds DispatchPermit until completion. Impact: timed-out requests can keep mutating state and consume dispatch permits after the client has been told the request failed. Acceptance: dispatch cancellation/timeout must propagate to the Cx used by dispatch_inner or otherwise drain/complete the blocking operation before returning; add a regression that proves a timed-out dispatch does not continue holding the permit or committing side effects after the timeout response.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:17:55.289764169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:20:38.887395610Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-atbuq","title":"concurrency: dispatch timeout leaves blocking tool work running","description":"Review finding in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs:9983-10055. HttpState::dispatch wraps asupersync::runtime::spawn_blocking in a hard timeout. asupersync spawn_blocking documents soft cancellation: dropping/timeout of the returned future discards the result but the blocking closure continues. Repro: configure a short HTTP request timeout, force a tools/call to block in SQLite/git work past hard_timeout_secs, observe the HTTP caller receives a timeout while dispatch_inner continues running and holds DispatchPermit until completion. Impact: timed-out requests can keep mutating state and consume dispatch permits after the client has been told the request failed. Acceptance: dispatch cancellation/timeout must propagate to the Cx used by dispatch_inner or otherwise drain/complete the blocking operation before returning; add a regression that proves a timed-out dispatch does not continue holding the permit or committing side effects after the timeout response.","notes":"Already implemented by NavySummit in commit 8f8dd5f0. Cooperative cancellation via DispatchCancel + run_cancellable_blocking_dispatch. Permit held until worker exits. Two regression tests included. Verified code review: approach is sound — permit prevents over-admission while cancellation signal relieves back-pressure.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:17:55.289764169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:31:18.183821543Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:31:18.183323811Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-ave","title":"Share: sqlite snapshot + project scope","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Steps 1–2: `create_sqlite_snapshot` and `apply_project_scope` with exact legacy semantics.

## Scope
- **create_sqlite_snapshot**:
  - Resolve destination path; create parent dirs.
  - Error if destination exists (never overwrite).
  - Open source DB; run `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(PASSIVE)` when enabled.
  - Use sqlite backup API to copy source → destination.
- **apply_project_scope**:
  - Load projects (id, slug, human_key); error if none.
  - If identifiers empty → no deletions.
  - Match identifiers case‑insensitive by slug or human_key; error on unknown.
  - Delete order per spec (agent_links, sibling_suggestions, messages + recipients, file_reservations, agents, projects).

## Tests
- Unit tests for identifier matching + error cases.
- Integration tests with temp DB and multiple projects; verify deletions and counts.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Save before/after row counts under `tests/artifacts/share/snapshot/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Snapshot creation refuses overwrite and produces byte‑identical DB backup.
2. Project scoping deletes only disallowed project data in the specified order.
3. Tests are deterministic and isolated to temp DB paths.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:05.665286231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:52:39.363640110Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:52:39.363620834Z","close_reason":"Implemented backup snapshot + scope parity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-ave","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-ax9xt","title":"atc_queries helper accidentally cfg(test)-gated blocks server test verification","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"TopazSummit","created_at":"2026-04-18T15:48:58.932760737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:23:30Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:23:30Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-b9k2","title":"T1.4: Wire am ci CLI subcommand with --quick/--report/--json flags","description":"## Objective
Expose the new CI engine via the `am ci` CLI surface with ergonomic and automation-friendly flags.

## Work
- Wire command routing for `--quick`, `--report`, and `--json` execution modes.
- Map CLI arguments to gate selection/report emission behavior.
- Implement clear exit semantics and user guidance for successful and failing runs.

## Deliverable
A production-grade `am ci` command entrypoint that operators can use instead of `scripts/ci.sh`.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is native Rust and cross-platform, introducing no new operational dependency on external shell/Python tooling.
- Preserves required legacy behavior (or documented intentional changes) with deterministic CLI/API semantics and stable `--json` output where applicable.
- Emits structured diagnostics and runtime telemetry sufficient for user-facing troubleshooting and CI artifact capture.
- Has comprehensive test coverage tied to this behavior (unit + integration + e2e where relevant), including negative-path assertions.
- Demonstrates reproducible behavior under CI constraints (timeouts, retries, deterministic ordering/seed controls).
- Produces explicit logging output suitable for rapid root-cause analysis when failures occur.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:24:19.275126556Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:58:44.766431913Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:58:44.766413449Z","close_reason":"Wired am ci CLI subcommand with --quick, --report, --json flags. Integrated with gate runner engine and JSON report generator.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-b9k2","depends_on_id":"br-2al9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1010,"issue_id":"br-b9k2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T1.4: Wire am ci CLI Subcommand

## What to build
Add a `Ci` variant to the CLI enum in lib.rs and handle it by orchestrating the
gate runner and report generator.

## CLI interface
```
am ci [--quick] [--report <path>] [--json]
```

Flags:
- --quick: Skip gates marked skip_in_quick (E2E suites). Always produces decision=no-go.
- --report <path>: Write gate report to this path (default: tests/artifacts/ci/gate_report.json)
- --json: Machine-readable JSON output to stdout (summary only, full report goes to --report)

## Behavior
1. Detect project root (git rev-parse or CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR)
2. Set up environment variables (CARGO_TARGET_DIR, DATABASE_URL, etc.)
3. Create output directories (mkdir -p for report path)
4. Run all gates via run_gates()
5. Generate report via generate_report()
6. Print summary to stderr:
   ```
   ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
     CI Summary: Pass=N  Fail=N  Skip=N
   ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
     Gate report: <path>
     RESULT: PASSED | FAILED
   ```
7. Exit code: 0 if decision=go, 1 if decision=no-go

## Implementation notes
- Add `Ci(CiArgs)` to the Cli enum in crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs
- CiArgs struct with clap derives for --quick, --report, --json
- The 13 default gates should be hardcoded as a constant (matching ci.sh lines 143-171)
- Per-gate output should be streamed to stderr in real-time (not just at the end),
  so the user sees progress. Use Command::spawn() + pipe rather than Command::output()
  for real-time streaming.

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs (Cli enum addition)
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/ci.rs (run_ci_command function)
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:28:11Z"},{"id":1011,"issue_id":"br-b9k2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## SECOND-PASS CORRECTION: T1.4 CLI Flags

The bash ci.sh supports these flags:
  --quick        Skip long-running E2E gates
  --report <path>  Write gate report JSON to specified path
                   (default: tests/artifacts/ci/gate_report.json)
  -h, --help     Show help

The Rust `am ci` should support:
  --quick          Skip gates marked skip_in_quick=true
  --report <path>  Specify JSON report output path
  --json           Print report JSON to stdout (NEW, not in bash)
  --gate <name>    Run only a specific gate (NEW, not in bash)
  --parallel       Run independent gates concurrently (NEW, T1.5)
  -h, --help       Show help

Environment variable:
  CI_GATE_REPORT_PATH — overrides default report path
  (bash: REPORT_PATH="${CI_GATE_REPORT_PATH:-tests/artifacts/ci/gate_report.json}")
","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:04:04Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-q0e0u","title":"Fix am robot subcommands rejecting canonically registered agents as agent not found","description":"Observed on /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust after registering CoralMarsh via canonical Agent Mail tools (macro_start_session / register_agent). Canonical MCP tools can resolve and use the identity: fetch_inbox(project_key, agent_name=CoralMarsh) succeeds, send_message from CoralMarsh succeeds, and file reservations for CoralMarsh succeed. But several CLI robot subcommands reject the same identity with: error: invalid argument: agent not found: CoralMarsh. Repros: AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli am robot status --project /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust --agent CoralMarsh --format json; AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli am robot inbox --project /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust --agent CoralMarsh --all --format json; AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli am robot reservations --project /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust --agent CoralMarsh --all --format json. Historical agent MistyRiver also reproduces. Notably, AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli am robot agents --project /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust --agent CoralMarsh --format json succeeds, so this appears to be subcommand-specific agent resolution/validation divergence inside the robot CLI path rather than missing project data. Investigate the robot read model and agent validation flow, make canonical tool-registered agents visible consistently across robot subcommands, and add regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GrayRidge","owner":"CoralMarsh","created_at":"2026-03-13T21:32:14.221354077Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T09:38:44Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T09:38:44Z","close_reason":"Fixed in da7e2f17: resolve_robot_scope now retries local DB when archive snapshot misses agent. Added is_agent_not_found_error helper + 2 regression tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1228,"issue_id":"br-q0e0u","author":"CoralMarsh","text":"Implemented source-side fix for robot agent resolution divergence: when explicit project/agent scope is missing in the local robot SQLite read model but present in the canonical Git archive, robot now reconstructs an archive-backed temp snapshot and retries resolution there. Also extended archive reconstruction to ingest file_reservations so reservations-dependent robot views can rebuild from canonical artifacts. Added regressions for missing-agent archive fallback and reservation reconstruction dedupe. Verification: rustfmt on touched files passed; rch cargo fmt --check passed; rch cargo check/clippy blocked by unrelated upstream Frankensearch compile errors after LockError::PolledAfterCompletion was added in asupersync (for example frankensearch-rerank/src/lib.rs:478 and frankensearch-embed/src/fastembed_embedder.rs:465).","created_at":"2026-03-13T22:03:40Z"},{"id":1230,"issue_id":"br-q0e0u","author":"CoralMarsh","text":"Follow-up verification detail on 2026-03-13: I also tried narrowing remote verification to the database crate to validate reconstruct.rs independently of the full workspace. Results via rch: (1) cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-db --no-default-features --lib --tests fails due pre-existing feature-gating problems in search_v3/query_assistance/search_response when tantivy-engine symbols are compiled out, and (2) cargo check / cargo test with --no-default-features --features tantivy-engine still fail before reaching the new reconstruct test because mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_service.rs currently references env_u64 in a configuration where it is not in scope (search_service.rs:2336 and :2342). These blockers are outside the touched robot.rs / reconstruct.rs paths, so current verification status remains: formatting passed, but compile/test verification is blocked by pre-existing unrelated search-layer breakage.
","created_at":"2026-03-13T22:11:22Z"},{"id":1234,"issue_id":"br-q0e0u","author":"CoralMarsh","text":"Fresh-eyes follow-up after the initial robot fallback patch:
- fixed archive fallback agent-profile detection to be case-insensitive when scanning archived `agents/{name}/profile.json` directories
- fixed a regression where `am robot agents` unnecessarily validated `--agent` through the new fallback scope path even though that subcommand only needs project resolution
- tightened the archive fallback regression to exercise a lowercase explicit agent name against a mixed-case archived profile directory

Verification status remains limited by unrelated workspace breakage. The latest targeted `rch` run for `cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-cli resolve_robot_scope_falls_back_to_archive_snapshot_for_missing_explicit_agent -- --nocapture` did not fail in the robot patch itself; it was blocked again upstream when the CLI crate pulled in Frankensearch and hit the existing `LockError::PolledAfterCompletion` non-exhaustive match in `frankensearch-embed/src/fastembed_embedder.rs:465`.
","created_at":"2026-03-13T22:43:54Z"},{"id":1235,"issue_id":"br-q0e0u","author":"CoralMarsh","text":"Fresh-eyes pass found and fixed an additional reconstruct.rs bug: archive reconstruction now accepts legacy/Python file-reservation artifacts that store the reservation path under 'path' instead of only 'path_pattern'. Expanded the reservation reconstruction test to cover both mirrored Rust-format and Python-format artifacts. rustfmt passed. rch-targeted cargo verification is still blocked before test execution by unrelated existing issues: upstream frankensearch-embed LockError::PolledAfterCompletion on default features, and existing mcp-agent-mail-db feature-gating/search_service env_u64 errors under --no-default-features.","created_at":"2026-03-13T22:55:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-q41fu","title":"Fix dashboard summary panels showing duplicated widgets, inflated latencies, and empty project/reservation data","description":"The Dashboard screen is rendering obviously incorrect operator state: tool latency panels report wildly inflated p95/avg figures for lightweight tools, project and reservation panels render 'No matching' despite active data across many projects, and lower dashboard widgets appear duplicated/repeated in the same frame. Diagnose each pipeline carefully, fix root causes in the dashboard/TUI code, add targeted regressions, and restore accurate live operator situational awareness.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-11T21:19:31.864970957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T09:50:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T09:50:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","metrics","projects","reservations","tui"],"comments":[{"id":1205,"issue_id":"br-q41fu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CoralElk taking a bounded dashboard investigation lane focused on crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/dashboard.rs first. Current repo state suggests br-t2x8z remains blocked upstream in sibling frankensearch, while this dashboard bead has no visible owner comment yet. I am avoiding the already-dirty tui_poller.rs/projects.rs surfaces until I confirm whether the root cause can be fixed in clean dashboard.rs rendering/query-state logic alone.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:33:12Z"},{"id":1209,"issue_id":"br-q41fu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CoralElk dashboard.rs slice landed. Changed the dashboard quick-query scope so live snapshot panels (agents/contacts/projects/project load/reservations) no longer blank themselves based on the free-form query text; query-specific hits remain in the dedicated query panel instead. Also made empty-state copy more truthful: e.g. projects now distinguish 'details unavailable'/'no projects' instead of always 'No matching...', and the same for agents/contacts. Remote verification via rch passed: env TMPDIR=/data CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_coralelk_q41fu_check cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib --tests. cargo fmt --check --package mcp-agent-mail-server is still blocked by pre-existing formatting drift in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/startup_checks.rs, which I left untouched because it is outside this reserved lane.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:43:46Z"},{"id":1210,"issue_id":"br-q41fu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Follow-up verification: targeted remote regression test passed with the same /data-backed rch path: env TMPDIR=/data CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_coralelk_q41fu_test cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-server snapshot_panel_query_terms_ignore_dashboard_quick_query -- --nocapture. This confirms the new helper semantics and the dashboard.rs slice compiles/tests cleanly on the server crate path even though full workspace quality gates remain noisy outside this bead.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:49:12Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-q9u9v","title":"perf-verify","status":"tombstone","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-19T00:14:09.507588431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:13:31.384352514Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:13:31.384352514Z","source_repo":".","deleted_at":"2026-04-03T01:13:31.384290809Z","deleted_by":"br-rebuild","delete_reason":"rebuild: not in JSONL","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-qiuzo","title":"concurrency: dispatch timeout can hold permit forever when blocking work ignores cancellation","description":"Repro:
1. Run a JSON-RPC dispatch through run_cancellable_blocking_dispatch with dispatch_timeout_secs=1.
2. Move a DispatchPermit into the blocking closure.
3. Make the blocking closure ignore DispatchCancel/Cx and sleep or block for an unbounded duration.
4. Observe the HTTP/dispatch response returns a timeout near 1s, but DISPATCH_INFLIGHT remains occupied until the non-cooperative closure exits.

Why this matters:
Commit 8f8dd5f0 correctly requests cooperative cancellation, but asupersync spawn_blocking cannot hard-cancel a running closure. The production dispatch path only checks the cancellation token before/after router calls unless downstream code cooperates via Cx. A non-cooperative DB/tool path can therefore retain the dispatch permit indefinitely after timeout and exhaust admission capacity.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add an adversarial test where the blocking closure ignores DispatchCancel after timeout.
- Prove bounded graceful degradation: either the permit is released by a hard grace mechanism within <5s, or the request is routed through a bounded worker mechanism that cannot permanently consume all dispatch capacity.
- Preserve timeout response latency near the configured dispatch timeout.
- Document the asupersync spawn_blocking cancellation semantics in the dispatch helper/test so future changes do not assume hard cancellation.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:35:57.053868805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:35:57.053868805Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","dispatch","server"]}
 {"id":"br-ql6bf","title":"Optimize agent-name normalization hot path","description":"Profile-driven optimization of is_valid_agent_name/normalize_agent_name to improve register_agent latency without changing accepted names or output normalization.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:35:24.334100447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T04:53:54.026627970Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T04:53:54.021005552Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-qni29","title":"T3.2: Email, broadcast, and descriptive name detection parity","description":"Ensure categories 3 (EMAIL_AS_AGENT), 4 (BROADCAST_ATTEMPT), and 5 (DESCRIPTIVE_NAME) detect exactly the same inputs with identical error messages. EMAIL: '@' in value and '.' in value.split('@')[-1]. BROADCAST: value.lower().strip() in {all, everyone, broadcast, *}. DESCRIPTIVE: contains dev/developer/agent/worker/assistant/helper/bot/admin/user/manager/lead/engineer. All keyword sets and messages must match character-for-character.","notes":"Implemented category-3/4/5 agent-name mistake parity in crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/models.rs. Added BROADCAST_TOKENS and DESCRIPTIVE_NAME_KEYWORDS, plus detectors: looks_like_email(), looks_like_broadcast(), looks_like_descriptive_name(). Extended detect_agent_name_mistake() order to include EMAIL_AS_AGENT, BROADCAST_ATTEMPT, DESCRIPTIVE_NAME with exact message text parity strings.
